'''Jedi brute''' was a title used by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] to describe one of four [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]-held ranks in the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] [[Temple Security Force|Security Force]]. Alongside the [[Jedi Gate Master|Gate Master]] and the [[Jedi Sniper|snipers]] and [[Jedi Temple Guard/Legends|Jedi Temple Guard]], brutes were the Temple's first line of defense from troublemakers, zealots, and rioters.

==Overview==
When a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] began their independent studies separate from their former [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]], they had the option of choosing from three classes of Jedi: the [[Jedi Guardian/Legends|Guardian]], [[Jedi Consular|Consular]], and [[Jedi Sentinel/Legends|Sentinel]]. A brute of the Jedi Temple Security Force was a Guardian who pursued the specialization of [[Jedi Peacekeeper|Peacekeeper]] based on Coruscant.<ref name="TJP" />

Stationed within the walls of the [[Temple district/Legends|Temple Precinct]], Temple brutes typically were physically intimidating and preferred to use [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed lightsabers]] when defending their home. Unlike the snipers, brutes worked in close-combat situations.<ref name="TJP" />

==History==
The rank of Temple brute dates as far back as the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformation]] and the reorganization of the Jedi Order by [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Fae Coven]]. Codifying the ranks and duties of the Jedi in her handbook called ''[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force]]'', the term was used until the dissolution of the Order in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. When the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] was revealed to be a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], he quickly ordered the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] to turn on the Jedi before they could remove the Sith from office. [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], the Sith Lord [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]' new apprentice, led the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] against the Temple, conducting [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|Operation: Knightfall]] as part of [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|Contingency]] [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]]. The brutes of the Temple defended it valiantly against the Sith and his army but were vastly outnumbered. By the end of the night, the brutes were largely, if not completely, routed.<ref name="Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith video game">[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith (video game)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' video game]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[Leland Chee]] describes the term "Jedi brute" as a gameplay term for a type of enemy, as opposed to an in-universe term used by the Jedi.<ref name="Chee">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=1335#16585930|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2007-05-12|time=4:53 PM}}</ref> This was later superseded by their inclusion in [[Daniel Wallace]]'s [[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']].<ref name="TJP" />
